hooks._comment_event_6: preToolUse — BEFORE any tool call (can block it)
hooks._comment_event_7: postToolUse — AFTER any tool call (can log, lint, format)

preToolUse._comment: RULE: Block edits to migration files, CI config, and hooks themselves

postToolUse._comment: RULE: Run linter/formatter on any Java file the agent edits
RULE: Log every tool action for audit trail

_comment_1: Hooks are shell commands that run automatically at key moments
_comment_2: during a Copilot agent session. They receive JSON via stdin
_comment_3: and can return JSON via stdout to control agent behavior.
_comment_4: Instructions = ADVICE. The agent reads them and usually follows
_comment_5: them, but it can drift. Instructions are soft guidance.
_comment_13: Hooks = ENFORCEMENT. They are deterministic shell commands.
_comment_14: A preToolUse hook that returns continue:false will BLOCK the
_comment_15: action every time, no matter what. The agent cannot ignore it.
_comment_18: - Protecting files the agent should NEVER touch → hook
_comment_24: Rule of thumb: if the consequence of ignoring it is serious,
_comment_25: use a hook. If it is just annoying, use an instruction.
_comment_40: TOKEN COST: Zero. Hooks run outside the LLM context.
_comment_41: Only the systemMessage (if any) adds a few tokens.
